mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate margin compression and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while housing starts remain elevated, it indicates synchronized real-sector strength that supports corporate revenue growth and validates equity valuations, driving positive forward market returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, corporate profit margins expand due to pricing power and volume gains, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, contradicting the expansion premium premise.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boost industrial sector earnings and lift the broader equity market within five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns remains statistically indistinguishable from zero across all rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.5%, borrowing costs exceed the sustainable threshold for highly leveraged corporate refinancing, triggering default risk repricing and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where the funds rate breaches the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle that sustains consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined payroll-unemployment signal yields a directional accuracy below 50% when tested against 60-day forward SPX returns in a walk-forward framework.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where core price indices stabilize while real credit conditions ease, equity risk premiums contract rapidly, generating positive short-term market returns driven by multiple expansion.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations where CPI stabilizes and RSAFS expands, falsifying the multiple expansion premise.
